scenario_results.reason: The lane progressed in-scope family-engagement work through explicit lifecycle state and closure evidence using child-care-specific records.
The lane blocked unsupported outreach until authorized-contact, language, and release prerequisites were retrieved.
Rework status was applied when referral and conference evidence was incomplete and the handoff remained unacknowledged.
The lane refused to mark work complete without the required conference, contact, and referral closure records.
Ambiguous ownership across teaching, family-services, and subsidy queues was surfaced and escalated instead of silently dropped.
The lane blocked the community-resource handoff until release authorization and required family-context artifacts were present.
A child-safety disclosure triggered immediate routing to the specialized authority rather than routine family-engagement handling.
Custody and subsidy questions were rejected as outside lane authority and routed to the correct owners.
The package escalated conflicting family-notification rules instead of inventing a public-versus-tenant policy synthesis.
Tenant adaptation preserved the child-care public baseline while recording that the operator used a non-Head-Start document set.
The lane used classroom, authorized-pickup, conference, screening, and transition terminology rather than generic support language.
Conflicting systems of record prevented unsupported closure and produced an explicit reconciliation or escalation requirement.

notes: All 12 scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry abilities are mapped and validated.
validated validation requirements are met with complete scenario and functionality coverage.
Runtime remains specification-based and no custody, subsidy, safety, licensing, legal, or clinical authority is claimed.
